title: Surgical Per-Bug Patch Series For Mainline Hardening
original_idea:
主题: 量化交易系统（GLD/SLV 期权 + 期货）架构鲁棒性与正确性优化

仓库根: /Users/yhdong/GoldDash (主代码, git repo) + /Users/yhdong/Gold (数据 + 训练, 非 git)

现状摘要:
- v3.7.232 期权交易系统, 主链: 信号生成 (signals_v2) → 策略选择 (BC/SP/STRADDLE/SHORT_VOL/FUTURES) → 仓位 ledger → 实时退出
- 多窗口回测两层: Layer1 信号验证 (10y/5y/3y/1y), Layer2 策略 P&L (1y/6m/3m)
- 刚加 expiry-intrinsic 强平兜底 (force_close_at_expiry), 仅 BC/SP 接入
- kline_db 滞后 17 天 (max=2026-05-06, today=2026-05-23), Moomoo 每日 100 额度
- Cross-asset: SLV-S → GLD BC 固定策略, 未 IV-aware

刚拿到的 Codex 架构审查结论 (5 严重 / 6 中等 / 4 优化):
严重:
1. paper_positions.py:689 simulate_option_exit 没传 asset 给 simulate_sp/bc/straddle/short_vol_position → per-asset cfg 全部失效, GLD/SLV 都用默认值
2. paper_positions.py:366 entry pricing 无 max_fallback_days, 用 stale kline 定价并冻结
3. v3.7.232 expiry 兜底只接 BC/SP, STRADDLE/SHORT_VOL 仍可能永远 OPEN
4. 生产 regime min_hold_days=20 (look-ahead), 回测=1, 不一致
5. Dashboard '真实策略回测' 走旧 run_backtest, 没应用 IV 三档/sp_score/MA filter/tier

中等:
- Cross-asset 应 IV-aware (GLD bp_low<=0.10 AND GVZ>=25 → SP)
- Layer2 回测只统计 closed → 幸存者偏差
- framework.py:120 max_move 反向 rolling 有 off-by-one
- OI 修正只在 Dashboard, 没进主链
- paper_positions.py:177 SP fallback sign 算反 (-1 应为 +1)
- positions_ledger.py:87 entry_spot 实际是期权 daily close 不是 ETF spot

优化:
- ledger daemon 加 kline 新鲜度硬闸
- 风控迁入主链 (exposure gate, max_open, 连亏熔断)
- v3.7.232 加回归测试
- yfinance fallback 用 bid/ask mid 优先

目标: 形成一份完整 idea draft, 系统性消除上述问题, 同时为后续 humanize 工作流 (explore-idea → gen-plan → refine-plan → start-rlcr-loop) 提供 grounded 起点。

约束:
- 一切要数据/事实说话, 不偷工减料, 不用模拟数据
- 数据必须当日更新, 不能用陈旧数据预测
- 优先消除正确性问题, 性能/优化次之
- 改动要可分阶段独立验证, 避免大爆炸式重构
synthesis_notes: The Primary series is intentionally minimal so each fix can be tagged, validated, and reverted independently — but it is not architecturally maximal. If the project later wants to consolidate, the alternatives fold in cleanly: Alt-1 (Signal Pipeline) absorbs v3.7.243 (Dashboard run_backtest deprecation) and rationalizes the 29 analysis scripts; Alt-2 (Typed Exit Context) absorbs v3.7.238 (asset threading) and v3.7.239 (Straddle/ShortVol expiry wiring) into one structural change; Alt-3 (Freshness State Machine) extends v3.7.236/237 (kline freshness guard) from a single-purpose gate into a full lifecycle observable; Alt-4 (Look-Ahead Audit) elevates v3.7.240 (max_move off-by-one) and v3.7.233 (regime min_hold_days) into a permanent enforcement layer; Alt-5 (Policy Layer) lifts v3.7.242 (cross-asset IV-awareness) into a YAML-driven engine that can host future risk rules without code churn. The recommended trajectory: Primary delivers correctness now in 12 tags, then v3.8 picks Alt-2 + Alt-4 together (typed context + leak detector form a coherent 'structural correctness' layer), and v3.9 picks Alt-1 + Alt-5 (one canonical pipeline driven by one declarative policy).

directions.name: Surgical Per-Bug Patch Series
directions.rationale: Treat each Codex finding as a standalone, independently verifiable patch (one file, one test) sequenced by severity — maximizes safety, traceability, and rollback granularity at the cost of more PR overhead than architectural rewrites.
directions.raw_phase3_response:
APPROACH_SUMMARY: Modular, version-tagged patch cadence addressing each Codex finding as independent single-file or tightly-scoped commits sequenced by severity. Each patch targets ONE root cause (1-3 files, minimal surface), includes a focused test, receives its own v3.7.* tag (continuing v3.7.233, 234, ...), and is independently reversible via git revert. Patch series ordering (severity + dependency): #4 regime default flip first (pure parameter); #2 max_fallback_days guard (defensive); then #1 + #3 + #5 grouped (all touch paper_positions.py exit logic). Affected components per patch: #1 paper_positions.py:689-700 + 4 strategy modules; #2 paper_positions.py:344-381; #3 options_exit.py + paper_positions.py:688-729; #4 regime.py default + app.py:112 + build_positions_ledger.py:92; #5 paper_positions.py:179 single sign flip.
OBJECTIVE_EVIDENCE:
- core/paper_positions.py:689-700 — 4 simulate_*_position calls without asset parameter
- core/strategies/buy_call.py:34, sell_put.py:42, straddle.py:22, short_vol.py:24 — signatures with cfg=None default
- core/paper_positions.py:344-381 pick_liquid_monthly_option — fallback_offset_days computed but never clamped
- core/strategies/options_exit.py:249-297 force_close_at_expiry only wired to BC (line 58) + SP (line 73)
- core/regime.py:24 default min_hold_days=20; backtest framework.py:70 forces =1
- core/paper_positions.py:179 sign = -1 for non-BUY-CALL/SPOT, applied to SELL PUT incorrectly
- Prior art: v3.7.184 per-asset SP split, v3.7.156 KLINE_DB mtime cache, v3.7.150 CSV corruption fix, v3.7.232 expiry intrinsic — all single-file patches with v3.7.* tags
- Test pattern: scripts/bc_entry_filter_test.py, scripts/test_oi_adjust.py, scripts/full_history_backtest.py (script-based integration validation; tests/ empty)
KNOWN_RISKS:
- Asset parameter threading depth — risk of incomplete migration silently re-introducing bug
- max_fallback_days clamp too aggressive — 7d may reject valid fallback during prolonged outages
- force_close_at_expiry signature mismatch — STRADDLE/SHORT_VOL differ in max_risk calc
- Regime min_hold_days=1 breaks intraday signal cadence — mitigated by EOD-only flip
- SP sign flip cascades — audit ledger/dashboard sign comparisons
CONFIDENCE: high
directions.approach_summary: Modular, version-tagged patch cadence addressing each Codex finding as independent, single-file or tightly-scoped commits sequenced by severity then dependency. Each patch targets one root cause, includes a focused validation script, receives its own v3.7.* tag (continuing the v3.7.150/156/184/232 cadence), and is independently reversible. 12 tags grouped into 6 phases: A) pure parameter/sign flips (regime min_hold_days, SP fallback sign, entry_spot rename); B) defensive ingestion guards (max_fallback_days, ledger daemon freshness gate); C) exit-simulation contract + per-asset cfg threading (asset parameter passthrough, Straddle/ShortVol expiry wiring); D) derived-metric corrections (max_move off-by-one, Layer 2 survivorship); E) cross-asset IV-awareness + Dashboard backtest parity; F) regression test harness (pytest, 12 expiry scenarios + per-asset cfg assertion).
objective_evidence: core/paper_positions.py:689–700 — simulate_bc/sp/straddle/short_vol_position called without asset/cfg parameter; signatures at strategies/{buy_call,sell_put,straddle,short_vol}.py declare cfg=None and silently substitute defaults — confirms Codex #1.
core/paper_positions.py:344–381 pick_liquid_monthly_option computes fallback_offset_days but applies no max_fallback_days guard — confirms Codex #2.
core/strategies/options_exit.py:force_close_at_expiry only called from buy_call.py:58 and sell_put.py:73; straddle.py:33 and short_vol.py:73 still go directly to first_kdb lookup — confirms Codex #3.

core/regime.py:24 default min_hold_days=20; _apply_min_hold (118–138) rewrites historical regime when chunk fails to persist 20 days; 11 production call sites use the default while scripts/backtest/framework.py:70 forces =1 — confirms Codex #4.
core/paper_positions.py:179 sign = 1 if strategy in ('BUY CALL','SPOT') else -1 applies to SELL PUT, which has positive delta — confirms Codex #5.
core/positions_ledger.py:87 stores entry_pricing['daily_close_price'] (option's own daily close) into a field named entry_spot — confirms middle finding.

Prior art for incremental v3.7.* single-file patches: v3.7.184 per-asset SP split (1 file + 1 validation script), v3.7.156 KLINE_DB mtime cache, v3.7.150 CSV corruption fix, v3.7.232 expiry intrinsic helper (3 files + 1 helper). v3.7.200–211 explicitly grouped 12 micro-fixes and each remained individually testable.

Test infrastructure: scripts/bc_entry_filter_test.py, scripts/test_oi_adjust.py, scripts/full_history_backtest.py all follow 'load historical CSV → apply patch → compare WR/sum/scoreB' pattern. tests/ directory currently empty — v3.7.244 introduces the first pytest suite without disturbing existing scripts.

CLAUDE.md:75-78 Workflow codifies '改 cfg → 跑 exit_grid_v2.py 验证 → backtest_pipeline.py all 自动归档版本 → kelly_analysis.py 仓位更新 → compute_strategy_stats.py 刷新 → build_positions_ledger.py 重建' — surgical patches integrate cleanly into this validate-then-archive loop.
known_risks: Per-asset cfg threading depth (v3.7.238): asset must reach force_close_at_expiry and every helper; incomplete threading silently re-introduces bug. Mitigation: assert asset in {'GLD','SLV'} at every dispatch site, run grep audit before tagging.
max_fallback_days clamp too aggressive (v3.7.236): 7d may reject legitimate entries during prolonged Moomoo outages. Mitigation: emit WARNING (not ERROR), allow MAX_KLINE_FALLBACK_DAYS env override.

SP sign flip cascading semantics (v3.7.234): downstream code reading realized_pnl_pct sign as a 'winning side' proxy may invert. Mitigation: audit app.py, notifier.py, compute_strategy_stats.py for direct pnl>0 comparisons on SP rows.

Regime min_hold_days=1 causes intraday jitter (v3.7.233): daily regime flips might trigger spurious filter changes. Mitigation: regime is EOD-computed; add 2-week shadow log comparing pre/post WR before declaring done.
Cross-asset IV-aware rule degrades sample size (v3.7.242): narrow regime has ~10-12 closed trades. Mitigation: BS-proxy backtest on 5y synthetic chain + 2-week shadow log.

Dashboard run_backtest deprecation breaks user workflows (v3.7.243): Streamlit pages may rely on output schema. Mitigation: keep as thin wrapper for one release with deprecation warning; remove in v3.8.
Validation script load on developer time: 12 tags × per-patch grid replay = real elapsed time. Mitigation: 'make validate-patch TAG=v3.7.238' so each patch's evidence is reproducible.

directions.name: Single-Source-of-Truth Signal Pipeline
directions.rationale: Multiple call sites bypass `generate_daily_signals()` (live ledger, layered backtest, dashboard `run_backtest`, OI-adjust display) — collapse them into one canonical pipeline so production/backtest/dashboard share identical filtering, regime, and tier logic, eliminating drift at the source rather than patching downstream symptoms.
directions.raw_phase3_response:
APPROACH_SUMMARY: Create SignalPipeline factory class in new module core/signal_pipeline.py with single build_signals() method accepting asset, OHLC, bands, regime, rv_pctile, optional gvz_series + force_apply_iv_filter + include_cross_asset flags. Returns canonical sig_df with metadata (iv_filter_applied, gvz_freshness_days, regime_classifier, generated_at). Collapses 4 refactoring targets: app.py:1286 (correct reference), app.py:4733 (add gvz_series), app.py:5301 (consolidate fetch), paper_positions.py:689-700 (pass asset). Phase 1 unification, Phase 2 paper_positions asset fix, Phase 3 cross-asset IV-aware, Phase 4 (opt) scripts migration.
OBJECTIVE_EVIDENCE:
- core/signals_v2.py:87 canonical generate_daily_signals already accepts asset, gvz_series — factory wraps not replaces
- app.py:4733 calls without gvz_series → disables IV filter (signals_v2.py:298-321)
- scripts/build_positions_ledger.py:94 + scripts/backtest/framework.py:82 already pass both asset + gvz_series — correct pattern not centralized
- 29 analysis scripts grep-found with inconsistent params
- 5 dataclass configs precedent (AssetConfig, BCConfig, SPConfig, StraddleConfig, ShortVolConfig)
- Dashboard 3 call sites all touch app.py — single migration target
KNOWN_RISKS:
- Streamlit hot-path performance — needs cache for sig_df
- 29 analysis scripts gradual migration
- GVZ data staleness handling
- Cross-asset rule temporal ordering (SLV before GLD)
- Test coverage gap (no formal pytest suite)
CONFIDENCE: medium
directions.approach_summary: Collapse 34 scattered call sites that currently bypass or re-implement parts of generate_daily_signals() into one canonical SignalPipeline.build_signals(asset, ..., gvz_series, force_apply_iv_filter, include_cross_asset) factory under core/signal_pipeline.py. Three Dashboard modes (app.py:1286, :4733, :5301), the ledger builder, the backtest framework, and 29 analysis scripts all consume this factory, making IV filter/sp_score/MA filter/regime/tier semantic drift structurally impossible. Includes optional metadata dict (iv_filter_applied, gvz_freshness_days, regime_classifier) so callers can introspect what the pipeline actually applied.
objective_evidence: core/signals_v2.py:87 canonical generate_daily_signals() already accepts asset and gvz_series — the factory needs to wrap, not replace.
app.py:4733 calls generate_daily_signals(...) WITHOUT gvz_series, silently disabling IV filter (signals_v2.py:298–321) for '回测分析' page.
scripts/build_positions_ledger.py:94 and scripts/backtest/framework.py:82 already pass both asset and gvz_series — the correct pattern, not centralized.
29 analysis scripts grep-found with inconsistent parameter sets.
5 dataclass configs (AssetConfig, BCConfig, SPConfig, StraddleConfig, ShortVolConfig) show precedent for typed parameterization.
known_risks: Streamlit Dashboard hot-path performance — needs sig_df caching.
29 analysis scripts gradual migration; risk of partial adoption.
GVZ data staleness handling — factory must degrade gracefully when gvz_series=None.
Cross-asset rule temporal ordering (SLV signals must be computed before GLD sync).
No formal pytest suite to validate factory output equivalence with current call sites.

directions.name: Typed Exit Context Contract
directions.rationale: The simulate_*_position functions silently take cfg=None and resolve sloppy defaults — replace each strategy's positional cfg with a single typed ExitContext dataclass (asset, signal_date, today_dt, kline_db, max_stale_days, cfg, regime), making 'missing asset' a structural impossibility and centralizing the expiry-intrinsic + stale-kline guards via type-discipline rather than per-callsite remediation.
directions.raw_phase3_response:
APPROACH_SUMMARY: Single typed ExitContext dataclass encapsulating asset, signal_date, today_dt, kline_db, cfg (union of BC/SP/Straddle/ShortVol configs), regime, max_stale_days, live_spot/high/low. Becomes sole entrypoint for all simulate_*_position functions. Eliminates cfg=None fallback — contract guarantees validated config. Refactor 4 strategy modules + dispatcher in paper_positions.py + force_close_at_expiry. 43 invocations across scripts/ + paper_positions.py + backtest utilities. Migration path: cfg=None|ctx=None coexistence for one release with deprecation warning.
OBJECTIVE_EVIDENCE:
- core/strategy_config.py:33 AssetConfig dataclass + 4 strategy module config dataclasses (BCConfig:17, SPConfig:16, StraddleConfig:13, ShortVolConfig:16)
- core/paper_positions.py:40 PaperPosition dataclass — existing structured-data pattern
- All 4 simulate_*_position signatures consistent: (entry_pricing, signal_date, today_dt, db, cfg=None)
- 43 invocations across paper_positions.py:691,694,697,700 + scripts/exit_grid_v2.py:240 + scripts/options_per_tier_validate.py:65 + backtest utilities
- core/strategy_config.py:230 get_config(asset) centralized — asset resolution infrastructure ready
- No mypy/pyright config — runtime assertions only
- Migration: ctx is not None → use it; else legacy path with deprecation
KNOWN_RISKS:
- 43 call sites — large surface
- Union type complexity for cfg field
- Breaking signature change
- kline_db pre-loading overhead
- max_stale_days threshold hardcoding (should be per-asset)
CONFIDENCE: medium-high (0.70)
directions.approach_summary: Replace each strategy's positional cfg argument with a single typed ExitContext dataclass (asset, signal_date, today_dt, kline_db, cfg, regime, max_stale_days, live_spot/high/low). Construction at the dispatcher (paper_positions.simulate_option_exit) requires all fields, making missing-asset/missing-cfg structurally impossible. force_close_at_expiry is rewritten to consume ExitContext, removing dependency on parsing asset from option code string. Migration via cfg+ctx coexistence for one release with deprecation warning.
objective_evidence: 5 existing @dataclass configs (AssetConfig, BCConfig, SPConfig, StraddleConfig, ShortVolConfig) plus PaperPosition show strong precedent.
43 invocations of simulate_*_position across paper_positions.py, scripts/exit_grid_v2.py:240, scripts/options_per_tier_validate.py:65, etc., would all migrate.
core/strategy_config.py:230 get_config(asset) — central asset resolution infrastructure already exists.
All 4 simulate_*_position signatures consistent: (entry_pricing, signal_date, today_dt, db, cfg=None) — uniform refactor target.
No mypy/pyright config in repo → contract enforcement is runtime-assertion-based; some leak risk remains until type checker is wired.
known_risks: 43 call sites — large refactor surface; risk of incomplete migration.
Union type for cfg field requires isinstance checks; static analysis won't enforce at construction.
Breaking signature change for any external consumers of simulate_*_position.
kline_db pre-loading overhead if callers don't coordinate; risk of double-load.
max_stale_days threshold should be per-asset (in AssetConfig), not fixed in ExitContext.

directions.name: Data-Freshness State Machine + UX Banner
directions.rationale: Silent staleness in kline_db / ETF OHLC / GVZ is the upstream cause of bug 2 and the v3.7.232 trigger — model the freshness lifecycle as an explicit FRESH/STALE/FROZEN state machine wired into ledger daemon + dashboard banner so failure modes are observable rather than hidden behind silent fallbacks.
directions.raw_phase3_response:
APPROACH_SUMMARY: Model data sources (kline_db, ETF OHLC, GVZ, OOS) as explicit FRESH/STALE/FROZEN state machine. New core/data_freshness.py module tracks per-asset, per-source freshness metadata. Integration: 1) _load_kline_db emit STALE if mtime > N days; 2) ledger_daemon (300s) checks state before invoking builder, records in positions_ledger_meta; 3) app.py startup populates sidebar banner (🟢/🟡/🔴) + dashboard snapshot augmented with data_sources block; 4) Entry pricing gate: pick_liquid_monthly_option max_fallback_days > 3 ⇒ return None + log; 5) Cross-asset GVZ STALE ⇒ skip or downgrade tier.
OBJECTIVE_EVIDENCE:
- data/positions_ledger_meta.json has evaluated_through + last_run_at — extension point
- core/paper_positions.py:320-341 _KLINE_DB_PATH + _load_kline_db with mtime caching (v3.7.156)
- core/paper_positions.py:349-379 pick_liquid_monthly_option has fallback_offset_days (v3.7.200) but no max threshold
- app.py:79-94 _data_mtime collects mtime tuples — pattern for freshness
- app.py:4670-4681 sidebar already shows 🟢/🟡/🔴 by age_min threshold — precedent
- core/dashboard_snapshot.py:1-121 has timestamp ISO SGT — extension point
- core/ledger_daemon.py:32-73 daemon rebuilds every 300s — integration point
- core/training_status.py:47-68 get_model_age_days + is_stale(7) — age threshold precedent
- /Users/yhdong/Gold/data/raw/options_history/kline_db/ max date 2026-05-06 — 17-day lag confirmed
- core/cross_asset_signal.py 28 lines, SLV-S→GLD BC — extension target
- No existing Enum/state machine class — novel addition
KNOWN_RISKS:
- Backward compat positions_ledger_meta.json schema
- Daemon write vs app read concurrency
- Per-asset configurable thresholds needed
- GVZ freshness multi-source
- Cold start no prior meta
CONFIDENCE: medium
directions.approach_summary: Model each data source (kline_db, ETF OHLC, GVZ, OOS predictions) as an explicit FRESH ≤ 2d / STALE 2-7d / FROZEN > 7d state machine wired into ledger_daemon and Dashboard sidebar. FROZEN ⇒ no new option entries (only MTM via force_close_at_expiry fallback); STALE ⇒ degraded confidence in sidebar; FRESH ⇒ normal. Snapshot file augmented with data_sources block listing per-source {state, last_update_date, age_days}. Entry pricing gate: pick_liquid_monthly_option max_fallback_days > 3 ⇒ return None with PENDING_KLINE flag.
objective_evidence: core/paper_positions.py:320-341 _KLINE_DB_PATH + _load_kline_db() mtime cache (v3.7.156) — precedent for mtime-based freshness detection.
app.py:4670-4681 sidebar already renders ledger mtime with 🟢/🟡/🔴 by age_min thresholds — direct UI precedent.
core/training_status.py:47-68 get_model_age_days() + is_stale(max_age_days=7) — age threshold pattern.
data/positions_ledger_meta.json already has evaluated_through per asset + last_run_at — extension point.
No Enum/FreshnessState class exists in code — new abstraction is required.
known_risks: Backward compat: positions_ledger_meta.json schema requires graceful fallback in readers.
Daemon write vs app read concurrency — needs read-only snapshot in app, write lock in daemon.
Threshold tuning is asset/strategy-dependent — needs per-asset configurable thresholds in strategy_config.py.
GVZ freshness has multi-source fallback (yfinance live vs CSV) — must distinguish live-fetch-failed from CSV-stale.
Cold start has no prior meta → all sources appear FRESH initially; needs backdated mtime check.

directions.name: Look-Ahead Eradication Audit
directions.rationale: The known regime min_hold_days=20 bug + max_move off-by-one + Layer2 survivorship bias suggest a class of leaks rather than isolated incidents — build a systematic look-ahead detector that, for every column in features_all + framework forward returns + OOS predictions, asserts the value at day t depends only on info-set ≤ t (or explicitly tagged fwd_).
directions.raw_phase3_response:
APPROACH_SUMMARY: Two-phase look-ahead detector: 1) Static analysis inventories all shift/rolling/expanding ops in src/features/, scripts/backtest/framework.py, core/signals_v2.py, core/regime.py. 2) Runtime validation: for each fold in walk-forward, assert feature[t] in info_set(≤t), OOS predictions trained strictly before test fold, labels prefixed fwd_ zero-padded NaN at end, regime min_hold_days=1 enforced, non-fwd_ columns with shifted(-h) flagged. Detector as pandas operation: detect_max_shift(col_definition).
OBJECTIVE_EVIDENCE:
- scripts/backtest/framework.py:108-134 next_open=shift(-1) [intentional], ext_close=shift(-(h+1)) [intentional], rv_fwd=rolling(h).std().shift(-h) [intentional], high_max=rolling(h).max().shift(-(h+1)) [off-by-one Codex flagged]
- /Users/yhdong/Gold/src/features/label_builder.py:43,68,102 — fwd_ret_*d, fwd_rv_*d, future_ret all clear fwd_ prefix
- scripts/setup_data.py:608-609 max_high_5d = shift(-1).rolling(5).max().shift(-4) = net shift(-5) — purpose unclear
- core/features_1h.py:314-315 fwd_high/fwd_low — integration audit needed
- core/regime.py:24 default min_hold_days=20 vs framework.py:70 forces =1; 11 production scripts inherit default
- features_all.parquet 92 columns — 0 prefixed fwd_ (correct separation)
- walk_forward_full.py:1-20 explicit no-look-ahead assumptions in comment but unenforced
- OOS train_dl_range.py:110-227 fold logic correct, dedup keep='last'
KNOWN_RISKS:
- Regime dual personality (prod 20 vs backtest 1)
- OOS dedup transitive leak (low risk)
- features_all lineage opaque
- max_high_5d intentionality
- features_1h not integrated
CONFIDENCE: high
directions.approach_summary: Beyond the known regime min_hold_days=20 bug and framework.py:120 max_move off-by-one, sweep every shift/rolling/expanding operation in core/signals_v2.py, core/signals.py, core/regime.py, scripts/backtest/framework.py, /Users/yhdong/Gold/src/features/, and core/features_1h.py. Build a runtime validator that for every column in features_all.parquet asserts the value at day t depends only on info-set ≤ t (or is explicitly prefixed fwd_). Walk-forward train/test boundary already validated; this adds enforcement as a permanent CI gate.
objective_evidence: walk_forward_full.py:1-20 already documents no-look-ahead assumptions in a comment but does not enforce them.
scripts/backtest/framework.py:108-134 contains next_open=shift(-1), ext_close=shift(-(h+1)), rv_fwd=rolling(h).std().shift(-h) — intentional fwd labels, all correctly named.
scripts/backtest/framework.py:120 high.rolling(h).max().shift(-(h+1)) has the off-by-one Codex flagged.
scripts/setup_data.py:608-609 high.shift(-1).rolling(5).max().shift(-4) = net shift(-5), purpose unclear — suspect mislabel.
core/features_1h.py:314-315 fwd_high/fwd_low lacks integration audit into features_all.parquet.
0 columns in features_all.parquet are prefixed fwd_ — features and labels correctly separated today, but enforcement is by convention only.
known_risks: Regime classifier dual personality: production min_hold_days=20 vs backtest =1 — backtest scorecards may not reflect production signal quality.
OOS predictions dedup may keep 'last' from a later fold; transitive leakage possible if fold overlap is large.
features_all.parquet lineage opaque — built once globally, not per-fold; intermediate rolling windows may not respect train/test boundary.
scripts/setup_data.py max_high_5d definition unclear — possible intentional target or possible misnaming.
core/features_1h.py not verified integrated into features_all.parquet pipeline.

directions.name: Declarative Risk & Cross-Asset Policy Layer
directions.rationale: Cross-asset rule, IV-aware strategy switching, consecutive-loss circuit breaker, and exposure caps currently live as scattered constants and inline conditionals — externalize all gate-keeping into a single declarative risk_policy.yaml evaluated by one policy engine, making 'what's allowed when' auditable, testable, and changeable without touching strategy code.
directions.raw_phase3_response:
APPROACH_SUMMARY: Externalize all gate-keeping into risk_policy.yaml evaluated by core/policy_engine.py. YAML contains cross-asset rules (SLV-S triggers w/ IV-aware), IV-regime strategy switches (GVZ<22 BC, 22-28 dual, >28 SP+bp_low≤0.10), entry filters (RV pctiles, MA, ret_20d, tier stratification), risk gates (consecutive-loss, max_open, exposure caps), leverage/tier rules (S=10× A=10× B=5×), expiry handling. PolicyEngine evaluates per signal, returns {entry_allowed, strategy_override, leverage, cross_asset_enabled}. Logs every decision. ~150 LOC YAML replaces ~400 LOC scattered Python.
OBJECTIVE_EVIDENCE:
- core/strategy_config.py:46-60, 87-97, 99-112, 126-135 — 8 major policy dimensions
- core/cross_asset_signal.py:29-83 — module-level constants CROSS_ENABLED, CROSS_TIERS, CROSS_STRATEGY
- core/signals_v2.py:24, 41-96 — CONSECUTIVE_STOP, BUY_BP, IV regime inline
- core/regime.py:23-28 — min_hold_days 20 vs 1 inconsistency
- core/strategies/options_exit.py:205-250 — expiry force-close only BC/SP
- core/paper_positions.py:630-700 — simulate_option_exit doesn't pass asset
- core/strategy_configs.py:51-89 — FuturesConfig tier-aware leverage precedent
- scripts/backtest/framework.py:227 — IV bp_low filter duplicated
- AssetConfig dataclass precedent (strategy_config.py:34-147)
- /Users/yhdong/Gold/config/settings.yaml — YAML infrastructure accepted
- 14 distinct gates, ~400 LOC scattered
KNOWN_RISKS:
- YAML schema lock-in versioning
- Rule interaction complexity
- Backtest-prod parity must enforce same YAML
- Performance if rules nest deeply
- Team adoption friction
CONFIDENCE: high
directions.approach_summary: Externalize all gate-keeping (cross-asset rules, IV-regime strategy switches, consecutive-loss circuit breaker, max_open_per_asset, exposure caps, leverage tiers, expiry handling) into a single risk_policy.yaml evaluated by a new core/policy_engine.py. PolicyEngine returns structured verdicts {entry_allowed, strategy_override, leverage, cross_asset_enabled} per signal, replacing 14+ inline conditionals scattered across signals_v2.py, cross_asset_signal.py, strategy_config.py, paper_positions.py, regime.py, options_exit.py. ~150 lines of declarative YAML replace ~400 lines of scattered Python branches.
objective_evidence: core/strategy_config.py:34-147 AssetConfig dataclass + ASSET_CONFIGS dict — per-asset parameterization precedent.
core/strategy_configs.py:51-89 FuturesConfig with tier-aware leverage (tier_s_leverage=10, tier_a=10, tier_b=5) — tier-rule precedent.
/Users/yhdong/Gold/config/settings.yaml + config.yaml — declarative YAML infrastructure already accepted.
core/signals_v2.py:24,41-96 CONSECUTIVE_STOP, BUY_BP, inline IV regime checks — concrete inline gate-keepers to externalize.
core/cross_asset_signal.py:29-83 hard-coded CROSS_ENABLED, CROSS_TIERS, CROSS_STRATEGY constants — canonical example to lift into YAML.
14 distinct inline decision points across 8 files (~400 LOC) — measurable consolidation target.
known_risks: YAML schema lock-in: once canonical, changes require versioning strategy.
Rule interaction complexity: 14 rules with correlated conditions may create subtle bugs.
Backtest-vs-production parity must enforce same YAML in both contexts.
Performance regression if rules nest deeply or depend on prior rule's output.
Team adoption friction moving from 'grep for feature' to 'read YAML'.
